column1: # Curated AADR "Group ID" -> deep ancestral component map.
# are ignored. ONLY listed groups become source-population references; everything else in
# the AADR is dropped. This file is the one expertise-driven artifact of the pipeline —
# review/extend it against the current AADR .anno labels and the source set you intend.
# Component codes should match (or be added to) the ancestry catalog in
# v66.p1-verified Group IDs (the labels above pre-date v66.p1 and mostly no longer match;
# auto-surfaced from the v66.p1 .anno — see pops/aadr_v66p1_ancient_groupids.tsv). These are the
# canonical, well-sampled deep references actually present in this release. Reviewed counts shown.
# ANF — Anatolian Neolithic (Turkey_N above already matches, n=68)
# WHG — Western hunter-gatherer (the Villabruna cluster). France_Mesolithic alone gives n=18, which
# works out to only ~6.8 called samples per panel site — far too sparse for a stable allele frequency
# (SE ≈ 0.19 at f=0.5). These are the v66.p1 groups that are unambiguously Villabruna-cluster WHG;
# DELIBERATELY EXCLUDED — each is a real hunter-gatherer population, just not *this* one:
#   · Serbia/Romania Iron Gates (n=73/10) — WHG–EHG cline; would drag WHG toward EHG
#   · any `-Dup` group (literal duplicate individuals — double-counting) or `-o`/`-oWHG` outlier
# Iran Neolithic (Iran_GanjDareh_N above already matches, n=17)
# CHG: no clean high-N Caucasus HG in v66.p1 (Kotias/Satsurblia are single genomes). Later/admixed
#   WHG–EHG-admixed. Resolved: excluded (see the WHG block above) — they would nearly double n but
#   at the cost of making WHG an EHG-shifted composite, and the three-way model has no EHG term to
#   absorb that. Keep WHG a clean Villabruna reference.
# ANE: only single genomes (n=1, e.g. Russia_MA1_HG.SG) — too sparse for a stable centroid.
# Natufian / Levant_N: Israel_Natufian (n=17), Jordan_PPNB (n=17) are strong deep refs but need a
#   new component code added to navigator-domain::ancestry before mapping here.
